Notice Of Application For Order Appointing Discovery Referee To Supervise Depositions
Off Calendar May-09-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DORIS CHENG OF THE LAW OFFICES OF WALKUP, MELODIA & SCHOENBERGER, TEL. NO. 1-415-981-7210 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. LINDSEY R. ADAMS-HESS OF THE BOWMAN AND BRROKE LLP, TEL. NO. 1-408-961-4540 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs' application for an order appointing a discovery referee to supervise the depositions of Toro witnesses is granted in part. The court appoints Everett Hewlett, Jr. of ADR Services as a discovery referee only for the depositions of Toro witnesses that are held in Minnesota. Unless Commissioner Hewlett or this court orders otherwise, all contact with Commissioner Hewlett must be by letter or email and all of his fees and costs will be paid by plaintiffs. Prior to the next deposition of a Toro witness in Minnesota, the parties are to provide Commissioner Hewlett a copy of the order appointing him, the moving, opposition, and reply papers on this application as well as the operative pleadings, but no other papers. The purpose of doing so is to provide sufficient information for Commissioner Hewlett to understand the background of this case in the event that he is called on to rule on any issues. The parties should notify Commissioner Hewlett of the dates and times of the depositions of the Toro witnesses in Minnesota and seek his guidance on how best to contact him in the event one or more issues arise at those depositions for which the parties seek a ruling from him. Appointment of Commissioner Hewlett as a discovery referee on the foregoing terms is warranted based on the showing of plaintiffs that past depositions of Toro witnesses in Minnesota would have benefitted from having a discovery referee available to resolve disputes that arose in those depositions. By requiring plaintiffs to pay all the costs associated with the discovery referee, subject to an order shifting those costs based on a finding that any conduct attributable to Toro lacked substantial justification, is fair and reasonable because there has been an insufficient showing that any of the Toro witnesses are likely to engage in conduct at their depositions which requires that a discovery referee be appointed. The court views this order as a prophylactic measure and is hopeful that Commissioner Hewlett will not be called on to make any rulings at any of the deposition of the Toro witnesses because counsel for the parties, who are obviously both skilled and thoughtful, will be able to work out all issues between them without need for Commissioner Hewlett. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Toros Person Most Qualified
Off Calendar May-09-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. DORIS CHENG OF THE LAW OFFICES OF WALKUP, MELODIA & SCHOENBERGER, TEL. NO. 1-415-981-7210 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. LINDSEY R. ADAMS-HESS OF THE BOWMAN AND BRROKE LLP, TEL. NO. 1-408-961-4540 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs' motion to compel deposition of person most qualified at the Toro Company is granted in part and their request for monetary sanctions is denied. On a mutually convenient date no later than May 26, 2017 defendant The Toro Company must identify and make available for deposition one or more persons to testify on behalf of Toro on categories 1-9, 12-13, 16, 17 and 22 in the notice of deposition on design issues. Plaintiffs have shown that a deposition on these categories meets the standard of discovery relevance and they are entitled to take the deposition of Toro, even though they have already deposed Toro design employees. Plaintiffs did not provide sufficient evidence in their moving papers to establish that information about the TX 1000 or incidents where Toro products other than the TX 525 meet the standard of discovery relevance. Because Toro's positions on this motion were substantially justified, no sanctions are awarded against it. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
